CHANGELOG.html.title: Changelog | MGM Character Controller
CHANGELOG.html.summary: Changelog All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. The format is based on Keep a Changelog and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning. [0.3.0] - 2026-03-18 Added Debug visualization system — Configurable debug display for contact points, movement paths, and collision impulses with per-entity color coding Move() method — Unified movement API with integrated debug visualization Configurable CollisionTolerance — Exposed collision tolerance and BodyType.Character detection on CharacterPhysicsProfile; added ContactType.Step Auto-generated assembly definitions — Editor scaffolding now generates runtime, authoring, and editor asmdef files with proper namespaces and GUID references ILocomotionSystem interface template — New DIM-based locomotion system interface generated by editor scaffolding, replacing inline boilerplate TestCharacter test state machine — Full test state machine under Tests/TestCharacter/ with runtime, authoring, and editor assemblies Changed ILocomotionState uses instance-method DIM pattern — State lifecycle methods are now instance methods with default interface implementations instead of static methods CharacterPhysicsProfile moved to ILocomotionContext — Decoupled from ILocomotionProfile so profiles no longer need to carry physics data Base authoring classes renamed — BaseCharacterAuthoring → BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring, BaseCharacterBaker → BaseCharacterLocomotorBaker Platform velocity inheritance — Replaced path-tracking system with MotionVelocity-based approach via ComputeInheritedDisplacement Post-collision impulse storage — Replaced NativeQueue<PostCollisionImpulse> with NativeParallelMultiHashMap for better parallel write performance Debug config inspector — Converted to UI Toolkit Locomotion system template simplified — Uses ILocomotionSystem DIM pattern; system struct now delegates to shared interface methods Generated file naming — All scaffolded files now prefixed with state machine name to avoid collisions across multiple state machines Test runtime namespace — Moved to WAYN.Games.MGM.Locomotion.Tests.Runtime Fixed Overlap resolution — Burst-compiled resolution for extreme overlap cases and low frame rate scenarios Dynamic body impulse scaling — Corrected impulse magnitude and platform velocity transfer Platform overlap and movement — Fixed characters clipping through or getting stuck on moving platforms Contact system and execute loop ordering — Overhauled contact detection; EvaluateTransition now runs once at the end of the loop Balance behavior — Fixed edge-balancing state detection Removed GenerateLocomotionSystem — Removed dead source generator method (superseded by DIM-based template) PhysicsProfileValue on ILocomotionProfile — Moved to context; profiles no longer carry physics configuration [0.2.0] - 2025-10-05 Added Support for custom state machine - Refactored systems to enable user defined state machine Source Generator - The package now relies on source generator to create the input and state machine execution systems. State Machine Creation Menu - Added a creation menu to set up a new state machine [0.1.0] - 2025-07-02 Added Modular locomotion architecture – Introduces a clean separation of concerns by splitting player input, character physics, and the visual model into distinct entities. This design supports both player-controlled and AI-controlled characters, and scales to multiple characters or players in local multiplayer scenarios. State-driven movement system – Character locomotion is governed by a flexible state machine with distinct states (e.g. idle, grounded, jumping, falling, sliding) and smooth automatic transitions between them. Movement behavior is defined by data-driven profiles, making it easy to tune speeds and jump parameters without code changes. Physics-based character controller – Uses physics under the hood for robust movement and collisions. Characters utilize a capsule collider and can step over small obstacles up to a defined height, as well as traverse slopes up to a maximum angle before sliding down steeper inclines. The system handles gravity and falling, inherits movement from moving platforms (so characters ride platforms smoothly), and includes solid collision resolution to prevent getting stuck on edges or corners. Integrated input handling – Built-in support for player input, character movement direction is calculated relative to the camera’s orientation, and an optional “aim” input allows the character to align with the camera direction for intuitive control. Input capture uses the new input system for easily supporting multiple control devices. Camera tracking for characters – A camera target tracking system automatically follows player characters to keep them in view. Integration with the new input system and PlayerInputManager allows natural support for split screen local multiplayer.The camera target follows smoothly the position and rotation in both single-player and local multiplayer scenarios. The solution supports use of Cinemachine cameras. Animation-ready character models – Visual presentation is decoupled from logic by spawning a separate model GameObject for rendering and animation. The locomotion system keeps the model’s transform in sync with the character’s state, enabling developers to hook up animations (e.g. running, jumping) to the character’s movement without affecting the underlying physics simulation. Customizable locomotion profiles – Character movement characteristics are driven by scriptable Locomotion Profile assets. These profiles define parameters like movement speed, acceleration, turning rate, allowed jump count, jump heights, and gravity settings for each character. This allows easy tweaking of locomotion behavior and per-character tuning by adjusting profile values in the editor. Example scenes – Included sample scenes demonstrate the locomotion system in action. A single-player example and a local multiplayer example are provided, showcasing basic player movement, jumping, camera follow, and multiple characters controlled simultaneously.

api/WAYN.Games.MGM.Locomotion.Authoring..title: Class BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile>.BaseCharacterLocomotorBaker<TAuthoring, TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TInput, TStateData> | MGM Character Controller
api/WAYN.Games.MGM.Locomotion.Authoring..summary: Class BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile>.BaseCharacterLocomotorBaker<TAuthoring, TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TInput, TStateData> Baker for the CharacterAuthoring. Converts the authoring data into one or more ECS entities with the following setup: Creates the primary character entity (manual‐override transform) and, if Playable is checked, adds a PlayableCharacter tag. If a ModelPrefab is assigned, spawns a separate “model” entity with: – PresentationGo (holds the prefab for rendering) – ControllingEntity (points back to the character entity) Applies the authoring transform to the character via LocalTransform. If a CharacterProfile is provided: – Generates or reuses a BlobAssetReference from the profile asset (and calls DependsOn so changes re-bake automatically) – Adds LocomotionInput to drive character movement from player input or AI Agent – Builds a CapsuleCollider from profile settings (CapsuleGeometry + CollisionFilter) and adds it as a PhysicsCollider blob asset – Registers the character with the physics world (PhysicsWorldIndex) – Adds LocomotionProfileReference (points to the blob asset), LocomotionData (initial velocity, jump count, etc.), LocomotionGravity (gravity multiplier) – Initializes the CharacterLocomotionStateMachine in the Falling state Inheritance object BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile>.BaseCharacterLocomotorBaker<TAuthoring, TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TInput, TStateData> LocomotionCharacterLocomotorLocomotionAuthoring.LocomotionCharacterLocomotorLocomotionAuthoringBaker TestCharacterCharacterLocomotionAuthoring.TestCharacterCharacterLocomotionAuthoringBaker Namespace: WAYN.Games.MGM.Locomotion.Authoring Assembly: .dll Syntax public abstract class BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile>.BaseCharacterLocomotorBaker<TAuthoring, TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TInput, TStateData> : Baker<TAuthoring> where TAuthoring : BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile> where TStateMachine : unmanaged, ILocomotionStateMachine<TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData>, IComponentData where TStates : unmanaged where TContext : unmanaged, ILocomotionContext<TProfile, TInput, TStateData> where TInput : unmanaged, IComponentData where TStateData : unmanaged, IComponentData Type Parameters Name Description TAuthoring TStateMachine TStates TContext TInput TStateData Methods Bake(TAuthoring) Declaration public override sealed void Bake(TAuthoring authoring) Parameters Type Name Description TAuthoring authoring ContinueBaking() Allows for additional logic or processing during the baking process of a character. Derived classes can override this method to implement custom behavior for extending the baking process. This method is invoked after the BaseCharacterBaker.Bake(TAuthoring authoring) method. The authoring component and different baking entities can be accessed through the protected fields of the baker. Declaration protected virtual void ContinueBaking()

api/WAYN.Games.MGM.Locomotion.Authoring..title: Class BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile> | MGM Character Controller
api/WAYN.Games.MGM.Locomotion.Authoring..summary: Class BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile> Serves as a base class for authoring character entities with locomotion capabilities. Designed to work with a profile of type TProfile, enabling customization of character movement and behavior through specific profile definitions. Contains shared properties and functionality for setting up entities related to character locomotion, including rendering models and defining state machines through further specialization. The class includes a nested abstract baker class used for converting authoring data into ECS entities with the following configurations: Associates the authored character with a profile asset (defining locomotion behavior and rules). Supports optional assignment of a prefab model entity for visual representation. Facilitates the integration of locomotion state machines, states, and custom contexts specific to the movement profile. Inheritance object LocomotorAuthoring BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile> LocomotionCharacterLocomotorLocomotionAuthoring TestCharacterCharacterLocomotionAuthoring Namespace: WAYN.Games.MGM.Locomotion.Authoring Assembly: .dll Syntax public abstract class BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile> : LocomotorAuthoring where TProfile : unmanaged, ILocomotionProfile Type Parameters Name Description TProfile The locomotion profile type derived from ILocomotionProfile. This profile encapsulates movement rules and parameters for the character. Fields PhysicsProfile Physics configuration (capsule dimensions, mass, slope limits) baked into a blob asset on the character entity. Declaration protected CharacterPhysicsProfile PhysicsProfile Field Value Type Description CharacterPhysicsProfile ProfileAsset The scriptable object that defines the locomotion profile for the character, specifying movement capabilities and behaviors in the game world. Declaration protected LocomotionProfileAsset<TProfile> ProfileAsset Field Value Type Description LocomotionProfileAsset<TProfile>

The class includes a nested abstract baker class used for converting authoring data into ECS entities with the following configurations: Associates the authored character with a profile asset (defining locomotion behavior and rules). Supports optional assignment of a prefab model entity for visual representation. Facilitates the integration of locomotion state machines, states, and custom contexts specific to the movement profile. BaseCharacterLocomotorAuthoring<TProfile>.BaseCharacterLocomotorBaker<TAuthoring, TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TInput, TStateData> Baker for the CharacterAuthoring. Converts the authoring data into one or more ECS entities with the following setup: Creates the primary character entity (manual‐override transform) and, if Playable is checked, adds a PlayableCharacter tag. If a ModelPrefab is assigned, spawns a separate “model” entity with: – PresentationGo (holds the prefab for rendering) – ControllingEntity (points back to the character entity) Applies the authoring transform to the character via LocalTransform. If a CharacterProfile is provided: – Generates or reuses a BlobAssetReference from the profile asset (and calls DependsOn so changes re-bake automatically) – Adds LocomotionInput to drive character movement from player input or AI Agent – Builds a CapsuleCollider from profile settings (CapsuleGeometry + CollisionFilter) and adds it as a PhysicsCollider blob asset – Registers the character with the physics world (PhysicsWorldIndex) – Adds LocomotionProfileReference (points to the blob asset), LocomotionData (initial velocity, jump count, etc.), LocomotionGravity (gravity multiplier) – Initializes the CharacterLocomotionStateMachine in the Falling state BasePlayerCharacterAuthoring<TInput> Abstract base class for authoring player character components. Provides support for player input assets and control over multiple characters. BasePlayerCharacterAuthoring<TInput>.BasePlayerCharacterBaker<TAuthoring> Baker class to convert PlayerAuthoring into ECS entity. CharacterFollowerAuthoring MonoBehaviour authoring component that marks a GameObject as a character follower entity. CharacterFollowerAuthoring.CharacterFollowerAuthoringBaker Baker that converts CharacterFollowerAuthoring into a CharacterFollower ECS component. CompositeEntityAuthoring Authoring component that marks a prefab root as a composite entity (Locomotor + Model hierarchy). Requires a LinkedEntityGroupAuthoring to be present on the same GameObject. CompositeEntityAuthoring.CompositeEntityAuthoringBaker Baker that converts CompositeEntityAuthoring to its ECS representation with manual transform override. LocomotionDebugConfigAuthoring Authoring component that adds per-character locomotion debug visualisation configuration. Attach to a character prefab alongside the locomotor authoring — fully optional. LocomotionProfileAsset<TProfile> Abstract ScriptableObject base class for locomotion profile assets. Concrete implementations populate the TProfile blob with authoring data during the baking process. LocomotorAuthoring Abstract base MonoBehaviour for all locomotor authoring components, used as a type-erased reference from follower authoring. MovingPlatformAuthoring The PlatformAuthoring class is responsible for configuring platform components and their associated properties within the Unity Editor. It is used to initialize platform behavior by baking it into ECS-compatible components, such as Platform, Rotor, and Track. PlatformAuthoring Authoring component that marks a GameObject as a tracked moving platform entity. PlatformAuthoring.PlatformAuthoringBaker Baker that converts PlatformAuthoring into a Platform tag component. PlayerGoAuthoring Most of the player components (camera, input, ...) still required MonoBehaviour. This authoring component has to be set on a game object prefab that will represent the game object side of the player and handle all MonoBehaviour interactions. PlayerInputAsset<TInput> The PlayerInputAsset is an abstract base class for defining player input behavior in a characters locomotion system. This class should be inherited to implement specific player input handling, such as mapping game inputs to a given input type. At runtime the asset is instantiated per player to allow caching of player specific reference (camera, etc...) in the asset instance. Structs BasePlayerCharacterBakingSystem A Unity ECS system that operates under the baking system filter. This system is part of the Locomotion.Authoring namespace and may contain logic related to the backing or processing of player character data during runtime. ControlledCharactersBakingList Temporary buffer element used during the baking process to store a list of entities representing controlled characters. This structure is utilized to transfer character entity references during authoring. Interfaces ICharacterLocomotionInput<TInput> Interface for character locomotion input components. Implement this on the TInput struct to allow accumulated input (e.g. jump triggers) collected each Update to be applied to the character during the fixed update step. Enums FollowStrategy Determines how the camera target follows the controlled character(s) each frame.

api/WAYN.Games.MGM.Locomotion.Runtime.Co.summary: Namespace WAYN.Games.MGM.Locomotion.Runtime.Components Structs CharacterFollower Registers an entity to follow a CharacterPath. CharacterPhysicsProfile Represents a physics configuration profile for a character, encapsulating core properties that define collision behavior, dimensions, and interaction with physics layers. CharacterPhysicsProfileBlob ControlledCharacters List of character entity controlled by the player or AI Agent Impact Describes a collision impact event between two physics bodies. LocomotionContact Last ground or obstacle contact for a Locomotor entity. LocomotionGravity Encapsulates gravity settings for the character. This value can be updated by a system to simulate any arbitrary gravity direction. LocomotionInputAsset<TInput> Stores a reference to the original PlayerInputAsset used by a player. LocomotionInputInstance<TInput> Stores a reference to the PlayerInputAsset<TInput> instance of a player. LocomotionProfileBlob<TProfile> Locomotion profile in the form of a blob asset. This profile contains configuration and parameters necessary for defining locomotion behavior within a state machine. LocomotionVelocity Locomotion velocity for a character locomotor. Motion PhysicsBody Lightweight reference to a physics rigid body, pairing its index and entity. Platform Tag component marking an entity as a moving platform tracked by the platform systems. PlatformMover Component driving a platform entity along a predefined track at a given speed. PlayerCameraHandle Component storing a reference to the player camera's target transform. PlayerGameObjectInstance Set of references to mono objects. These are used to update and managed game object behaviors of the player entity from systems allowing for greater control on the player loop. PlayerGameObjectPrefab Set of references to mono objects. These are used to update and managed game object behaviors of the player entity from systems allowing for greater control on the player loop. PostCollisionImpulse Represents the impulse applied to a rigid body following a collision event. Rotor Component marking an entity as a rotating platform, providing its rotation axis. Track Buffer element defining one waypoint position on a platform track. Enums BodyType Classifies the physics body type of a collision contact surface. ContactType Classifies the geometry of the collision contact surface. InterpolationMode Controls whether the character's transform is updated via physics graphical smoothing interpolation.

This is picked up by a source generator to generate the corresponding state machine dispatch. LocomotionStateMachineAttribute This attribute mark an enum as a Locomotion state machine. This is picked up by a source generator to generate the corresponding state machine component and systems. Structs BaseLocomotionContext Base context available to all locomotion state machine. Contains the minimal core state data (Velocity, Position, ...) and information (Entity, Collider, PhysicsWorld,...) required for locomotion state logic and platform handling logic. LocomotionJob<TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData> Burst-compiled ECS chunk job that drives the full locomotion execute loop for every Locomotor entity in a chunk: inherit displacement, solve overlaps, handle impulses, integrate, collide-and-slide, snap-to-ground, and evaluate transitions. LocomotionStateBehaviorDispatcher Static dispatcher that invokes ILocomotionState<TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData> lifecycle methods on a zero-size default instance of TState. Burst devirtualises each call at compile time — no boxing occurs. Interfaces ILocomotionContext<TProfile, TInput, TStateData> Interface enforcing the minimal information need for the context of locomotion state machine. It enforces the encapsulation configuration, input, and state data. ILocomotionProfile Marker interface for locomotion profile data. Implement this interface on an unmanaged struct that holds per-profile configuration values (e.g. movement speed, jump height) baked into a blob asset and supplied to the locomotion job. ILocomotionStateMachine<TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData> Interface that defines the structure of a character locomotion state machine, responsible for managing state transitions, physics integration, collision resolution, and other locomotion behaviors of characters. ILocomotionState<TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData> Interface for defining locomotion states within a character movement state machine. Enums OverlapState

CharacterContactSystem.Singleton Singleton holding the per-frame contact data maps produced by CharacterContactSystem. CharacterFollowerSystem A system responsible for updating the transformations of entities that follow a character within the game world. CharacterFollowerSystem.CharacterFollowerJob Burst-compiled parallel job that copies the followed entity's LocalToWorld to each follower. ILocomotionSystem<TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData>.LocomotionJobData Aggregated data bag holding all component type handles and lookups required by the locomotion job. PlatformMovementJob Burst-compiled parallel job that moves platform entities along their configured track waypoints. PlatformMovementSystem This system updates the position and rotation of platforms. PlatformRotateJob Burst-compiled parallel job that rotates platform entities around their configured axis each frame. PlayerGoManagerSystem Manages the lifecycle of the player game object. It ensures it's instantiation from the prefab for every player. When a player entity gets destroyed, the cleanup component ensures the destruction of the camera rig game object. PlayerGoManagerSystem.CleanUpControlledPlayerCharacterJob Parallel job that removes destroyed character entities from each player's ControlledCharacters buffer. PostCollisionImpulseClearJob Burst-compiled job that clears the post-collision impulse map after all impulses have been applied. PostCollisionImpulseSystem ECS system that dequeues and applies post-collision impulses accumulated during the locomotion job. Runs after the physics group to ensure all collision responses have been resolved. PostCollisionImpulseSystem.Singleton Singleton component holding the shared post-collision impulse map. Interfaces ILocomotionSystem<TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData> Interface implemented by generated locomotion systems. Provides the contract for scheduling and running the LocomotionJob<TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData> each fixed update step.
